data.title: Swvl launches operations in Kuwait with $2.2M contract
data.description:
• Swvl (SWVL) launched operations in Kuwait, securing a $2.2M multi-year contract to deploy its full stack of enterprise mobility solutions.
  • The rollout builds on strong momentum across the region, following successful launches and long-term collaborations in the UK, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Egypt.
  • The company said that it continues to see strong traction from sectors such as logistics, manufacturing, retail, and large corporate campuses across the region.

data.title: S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Index rises more than expected Y/Y in November
data.description:
November S&P Cotality Case-Shiller Home Price Index for 20 cities (seasonally adjusted): +0.5% M/M vs. +0.3% in October, according to data released on Tuesday.

+1.4% Y/Y vs. +1.2% consensus and +1.3% prior.

20-city index (unadjusted): 0.0% M/M vs. -0.3% prior.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: CoStar Group gains as activist Third Point confirms proxy fight
data.description:
CoStar Group (CSGP) rose 5.4% in premarket after activist hedge fund Third Point unveiled plans to start a campaign to push for new directors and restructure operations.

Third Point is preparing to nominate several directors to CoStar's eight-person board, according to a letter the activist sent to the board on Tuesday.

Third Point, which is headed by Dan Loeb, wants the real estate data company to focus on its commercial business and shut down or sell its residential operation, according to the letter. The reconstituted board should immediately consider strategic alternatives for Homes.com and related RRE businesses.

"CoStar stands at a critical inflection point," Loeb wrote in the letter. "Years of strategic blunders, uncontrolled spending, and board inaction have eroded shareholder trust. Immediate, decisive action is required to halt further value destruction and rebuild investor confidence. We look forward to engaging with our fellow long-term shareholders to save CoStar."

The letter comes just hours after a standstill agreement, which was signed last year by Third Point, expired at midnight.

CoStar didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment. News of the activist campaign was earlier reported by Reuters.

data.title: FHFA house price gauge rises more than expected in November
data.description: The Federal Housing Finance Agency House Price Index rose 0.6% M/M to 439.3 in November, exceeding the +0.3% consensus and the prior month's 0.4% increase, according to data released on Tuesday.

data.title: Applied Industrial Technologies raises dividend by 10.9% to $0.51
data.description:

data.title: Royce Micro-Cap Trust reports NAV of $11.79 as of Dec. 31, 2025
data.description:
Royce Micro-Cap Trust (NYSE:RMT) reports NAV of $11.79, while the market price at the close of Dec. 31, 2025, was $10.43.

The fund maintained an average weekly trading volume of 429,716 shares.

data.title: Royce Small-Cap Trust reports NAV of $17.69 as of Dec. 31, 2025
data.description:
Royce Small-Cap Trust (RVT) reports NAV of $17.69, while the market price at the close of Dec. 31, 2025, was $16.10.

The fund continues to demonstrate robust liquidity with an average weekly trading volume of nearly 1.5 million shares.

data.title: Aurora Mobile partners with Shenzhen stock exchange for information delivery
data.description:
• Aurora Mobile (JG) announced a strategic partnership with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange to leverage Aurora's JPush intelligent push notification solution to upgrade SZSE's digital infrastructure.
  • The deal focuses on enhancing SZSE's mobile platform for faster, more reliable delivery of critical market data.
  • Aurora's platform will serve as a vital channel for the timely delivery of regulatory updates, corporate filings, and investor education content across the market.
  • The stock price traded about 4% higher on Tuesday during early hours.
